name: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS)
description: Software does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output used as a web page

name: SQL Injection
description: Software constructs SQL commands from user input without proper neutralization, allowing attacker-controlled queries

name: OS Command Injection
description: Software constructs OS commands from user input without proper neutralization

name: Path Traversal
description: Software uses external input to construct a pathname intended to identify a file or directory under a restricted parent, but does not properly neutralize special elements

name: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: Web application does not sufficiently verify that a well-formed, valid, consistent request was intentionally provided by the user

name: Improper Authentication
description: Software does not sufficiently authenticate the claimed identity of an actor

name: Missing Authorization
description: Software does not perform authorization checks when an actor attempts to access a resource or perform an action

name: Incorrect Authorization
description: Software performs authorization checks incorrectly, allowing bypass of intended access restrictions

name: Exposure of Sensitive Information
description: Software exposes sensitive information to an actor not authorized to have access to that information

name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
description: Software deserializes untrusted data without verifying that the resulting data will be valid

name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: Web server receives a URL from upstream and retrieves contents without ensuring the request is being sent to the expected destination

name: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
description: Software does not perform authentication for functionality that requires a provable user identity

name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
description: Software contains hard-coded credentials for inbound or outbound authentication

name: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
description: Software transmits or stores authentication credentials in a manner that allows interception or retrieval

name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference (XXE)
description: Software processes XML input with external entity references that can be abused to disclose files or perform SSRF

name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
description: Software allows the attacker to upload or transfer files of dangerous types that can be automatically processed

name: Improper Privilege Management
description: Software does not properly assign, modify, track, or check privileges for an actor

name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
description: Software specifies permissions for a security-critical resource in a way that allows unintended actors to access it

name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
description: Software uses a broken or risky cryptographic algorithm or protocol

name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
description: Software transmits sensitive data in cleartext over a communication channel that can be sniffed

name: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
description: Software stores sensitive information in cleartext within a resource that might be accessible to another control sphere

name: Use After Free
description: Software references memory after it has been freed, which can cause a program crash or execution of arbitrary code

name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input (Buffer Overflow)
description: Software copies input to a buffer without checking that the size of the input does not exceed the buffer capacity

name: Improper Control of Generation of Code (Code Injection)
description: Software constructs code segments using externally-influenced input without proper neutralization

name: Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes (Prototype Pollution)
description: Software modifies or allows modification of properties on object prototypes, affecting all objects of that type
